big_picture: Asia stocks slide as US and Iran threaten to escalate war. The rupee hit a record low at 93.84 against the US dollar as the US-Iran war boosted crude oil prices. Major central banks have paused rate cuts due to the West Asian conflict, with the RBI facing a weakening rupee and risks to inflation, growth, and the current account. The 2026 Assembly elections schedule has been announced for Assam, West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Puducherry. The Corporate Amendment Bill will be tabled in the Lok Sabha today, focusing on easier compliance and stricter digital checks.

story_clusters.summary: The Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gyanesh Kumar announced on March 15, 2026, that the 2026 State Assembly elections will be held in Assam, West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Puducherry. Reports highlight political developments in Assam, including discontent among BJP leaders over recent Congress recruits and broader electoral dynamics. Tamil Nadu is also mentioned as one of the states set to hold elections in 2026.

articles.title: Car prices are set to rise up to 2% from April 1 — Here's why
articles.summary: Car prices in India are expected to rise by up to 2% starting April 1, due to a combination of factors including a general price hike and a 1% increase in road tax on CNG vehicles in Maharashtra. The Maharashtra government's decision specifically targets CNG vehicle owners, making the impact sharper in the state. The price increase reflects broader automotive industry trends and state-level fiscal policies affecting vehicle costs.
articles.raw_summary: Car prices are set to rise by 2% from April 1. In Maharashtra, the impact will be sharper for CNG buyers, as the state implements a 1% increase in road tax on CNG vehicles.

articles.title: RBI approves the voluntary amalgamation of Lalbaug Co-operative Bank Ltd., Vadodara, Gujarat with Akhand Anand Co-operative Bank Ltd., Surat, Gujarat
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India, in exercise of the powers conferred under sub-section (4) of Section 44A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of Lalbaug Co-operative Bank Ltd., Vadodara, Gujarat with Akhand Anand Co-operative Bank Ltd., Surat, Gujarat. The Scheme will come into effect from March 23, 2026 (Monday). All the branches of Lalbaug Co-operative Bank Ltd., Vadodara, Gujarat will function as branches of Akhand Anand Co-operative B

articles.title: Many In Judiciary Suffer From 'More Loyal Than King' Syndrome : Justice Ujjal Bhuyan

This move aims to maintain regulatory oversight during gaps in state-level environmental governance.

articles.title: Global Market Update | China, Hong Kong indices plunge over 3%, headed for worst day in a year
articles.summary: Asian stock markets, including China and Hong Kong, fell over 3%—their worst drop in nearly a year—amid fears of stagflation triggered by the Middle East conflict. Investors pulled out of tech and travel sectors due to concerns over higher oil prices and reduced demand. Energy and electric vehicle stocks gained as countries prioritize energy security. The volatility reflects broader economic uncertainty that could impact global trade and energy policies.
articles.raw_summary: Asian markets experienced their worst day in nearly a year as the Middle East conflict fueled stagflation fears and market volatility. Investors divested from sectors like tech and travel, anticipating higher oil prices and reduced demand. Conversely, energy and electric vehicle stocks saw gains amid a push for energy security.

articles.title: Oil Price Today (March 23): Crude oil steadies above $110 as Middle East tensions send mixed signals. What lies ahead?
articles.summary: Oil prices remain steady above $110 per barrel as tensions between the US and Iran create uncertainty in the Middle East. Brent crude is trading near $111, while West Texas Intermediate is around $98. Analysts warn prices could rise further if the conflict escalates, potentially reaching $120 or $150 per barrel. The situation involves global oil markets and geopolitical risks that may affect India's energy imports and economic stability.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices are holding steady. Investors are watching threats between the US and Iran. Potential Iranian oil supply is expected. Brent crude is near $111 per barrel. West Texas Intermediate is around $98. Prices could rise further if the conflict continues. Experts predict Brent could reach $120 or even $150 per barrel.

articles.title: Tipco Engineering IPO opens: Check GMP, price band, subscription and other details
articles.summary: Tipco Engineering's IPO opened on Monday, aiming to raise Rs 61 crore with a flat grey market premium, indicating investor focus on fundamentals. The company, a manufacturer of industrial machinery, will use the proceeds for debt repayment and working capital. The issue is priced between Rs 84-89 per share and closes March 25.
articles.raw_summary: Tipco Engineering's IPO opened on Monday, aiming to raise Rs 61 crore with a flat grey market premium, indicating investor focus on fundamentals. The company, a manufacturer of industrial machinery, will use the proceeds for debt repayment and working capital. The issue is priced between Rs 84-89 per share and closes March 25.

articles.title: Gold slides over 3% as Middle East tensions stoke inflation fears
articles.summary: Gold prices fell over 3% to a four-month low on Monday as Middle East tensions escalated, fueling inflation fears and expectations of higher global interest rates. Iran threatened energy systems, while oil prices remained high, increasing transport and manufacturing costs. The situation may influence India's energy import strategy and inflation management policies.
articles.raw_summary: Gold prices tumbled over 3% on Monday, hitting a four-month low. Escalating Middle East tensions fueled inflation worries and boosted expectations for higher global interest rates. Iran threatened energy systems, while oil prices remained elevated. This situation impacts transport and manufacturing costs.

articles.title: Dollar poised for rally as escalating Middle East conflict spurs haven demand
articles.summary: The U.S. and Iran are escalating threats of retaliatory strikes, including potential attacks on infrastructure, amid rising Middle East tensions. This has increased demand for safe-haven assets like the dollar, while rising energy prices are prompting central banks to adopt stricter monetary policies. The situation could impact global oil prices and economic stability, with potential indirect effects on India's energy imports and inflation.
articles.raw_summary: The dollar strengthened as Middle East tensions escalated, driving demand for safe-haven assets. Retaliatory threats between the U.S. and Iran, including potential strikes on infrastructure, have dimmed hopes for de-escalation. This geopolitical uncertainty is prompting central banks to adopt a more hawkish stance due to rising energy prices.

articles.title: Global Market Today | Asia shares slip, oil choppy as Gulf war escalates
articles.summary: Global markets fell as rising US-Iran tensions and the Israel conflict drove oil prices higher. Asian shares and futures dropped, with investors expecting prolonged fighting. This surge in oil prices threatens inflation control efforts, likely pushing central banks to raise interest rates and weakening bond markets. The US dollar strengthened as a safe investment during the uncertainty.
articles.raw_summary: Global markets tumbled as escalating US-Iran tensions and prolonged Israeli conflict sent oil prices soaring. Asian shares and futures dipped, with investors bracing for weeks of fighting. This inflationary shock has extinguished hopes for rate cuts, pushing central banks towards hikes and hammering bonds. The dollar strengthened as a safe haven.

articles.title: World could face worst energy crisis in decades, warns IEA chief Fatih Birol
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) chief Fatih Birol warned that escalating conflicts in West Asia have disrupted global oil supplies, potentially leading to the worst energy crisis in decades. This disruption threatens the global economy and could impact India's energy security, given its reliance on oil imports. Birol emphasized the need for urgent attention to stabilize energy markets.
articles.raw_summary: Birol warned that escalating tensions and conflict in West Asia have significantly disrupted global oil supplies, posing a serious threat to the global economy.

articles.title: Gold, Silver prices drop over 6% on MCX, extend losses in 'bear-market zone' amidst global sell-off
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) have fallen over 6% amid a global sell-off triggered by the US-Iran conflict in West Asia. The rising oil prices from the conflict have heightened inflation concerns worldwide. The decline extends losses, pushing prices into a 'bear-market zone'. Analysts attribute the drop to broader market uncertainty and risk aversion.
articles.raw_summary: Gold and Silver have remained under pressure ever since the start of the US-Iran war in West Asia, which has sent oil prices soaring and stoked inflation fears across the globe.

articles.title: West Asia war: US issues global security alert, warns Americans of possible threats from pro-Iran groups
articles.summary: The US issued a global security alert warning American citizens of possible threats from groups aligned with Iran. The advisory suggests these groups may target US-linked sites worldwide, including regions where India has strategic interests. This situation could impact India's foreign policy and regional security considerations.
articles.raw_summary: The US advisory for American citizens warned that groups aligned with Iran could attempt to target sites connected with the US or its citizens in various regions across the globe.

articles.title: Iran threatens to 'completely' shut Strait of Hormuz after Trump’s warning
articles.summary: Iran has threatened to fully close the Strait of Hormuz after a warning from former US President Donald Trump. The strait is a vital shipping route for global oil supplies. US-Israeli strikes on Iran could further destabilize the region and disrupt energy routes. This situation may have implications for India's energy security and regional stability.
articles.raw_summary: Iran warns it will fully close the Strait of Hormuz after US Donald Trump ultimatum, as US Israeli strikes on Iran threaten global energy routes and regional stability.

articles.summary: Generic versions of the diabetes drug Semaglutide have been launched in India at significantly lower prices, ranging from ₹1,290 to ₹8,000 per month, compared to ₹8,800 to ₹16,400 for the original drug. Analysts suggest this could benefit certain pharma stocks. The price reduction may improve affordability and access to the medication for patients in India.

articles.title: Iran-US-Israel War Live Updates: Sirens blared in Israel's ; China refiners eye Iranian oil
articles.summary: Escalating conflict between Iran, the US, and Israel has raised concerns about potential disruptions to oil supplies from the Strait of Hormuz. Iran warned it may shut the Strait completely and target regional infrastructure after US President Donald Trump threatened strikes on Iranian power plants. Chinese refiners are reportedly considering increased purchases of Iranian oil amid the tensions.

articles.title: Saudi Aramco boss pulls out of major international energy conference due to Iran conflict
articles.summary: Saudi Aramco's CEO Amin Nasser skipped the CERAWeek energy conference in Houston due to escalating conflicts involving Iran. Attacks on key energy sites in Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, and Qatar have disrupted oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, causing volatility in global oil markets. This situation may impact India's energy imports and could prompt policy responses to ensure supply stability.
articles.raw_summary: Amin Nasser skips CERAWeek in Houston amid Iran conflict as attacks on Aramco, SAMREF, Ras Laffan and Kuwait energy sites roil Strait of Hormuz and oil markets.

articles.title: Crude oil prices ease but remain at elevated levels despite Trump, Iran trading barbs
articles.summary: Brent crude oil prices remain elevated, surging over 50% since the start of the Russia-Ukraine war, with volatility driven by shifting US policies toward Iran. The US has alternately considered escalating military action and winding down efforts, contributing to uncertainty in global oil markets. This volatility impacts India's fuel costs, inflation, and trade dynamics, potentially prompting policy responses.
articles.raw_summary: Brent, the global benchmark, has surged more than 50% since the start of the war, that has now entered its fourth week, with no signs of easing. Prices are also volatile due to the constant flip-flop by Trump, who, before issuing his 48-hour ultimatum, said that the US is considering "winding down" the military efforts against Iran.

articles.title: Erasure, not inclusion: Why activists are opposing amendments to Transgender Act
articles.summary: Activists are opposing proposed amendments to India's Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019, arguing that the changes would lead to erasure rather than inclusion of transgender rights. The amendments, which are under discussion, have drawn criticism from transgender communities and allies who claim the revisions weaken protections. The article highlights demands for policy action to ensure the rights and welfare of transgender individuals are upheld.

The top court ruled that this procedural error invalidated the entire case. The decision highlights the importance of proper legal procedures in police investigations and court proceedings.

articles.title: 'Crisis worse than two 1970s oil shocks put together': IEA chief's big warning on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned of an unprecedented energy crisis, describing it as worse than the combined oil shocks of the 1970s. The crisis stems from damaged energy infrastructure and threats to the Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for global oil and gas shipments. The IEA emphasizes the need for international cooperation to prevent widespread fuel shortages and stabilize the global economy. The situation could have significant implications for India's energy security and po
articles.raw_summary: The world faces an unprecedented energy crisis, a potent mix of oil and gas shocks, warns the International Energy Agency. With infrastructure damaged and the vital Strait of Hormuz threatened, global economic stability is at risk. The agency stresses the need for international cooperation to resolve the escalating situation and prevent widespread fuel shortages.

articles.title: Vedanta shares plunge 5% as SC rules conglomerate not entitled to concessional rates for high-speed diesel
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Vedanta is not entitled to concessional rates for high-speed diesel under Form C, as the company allegedly misused the benefit beyond mining purposes. Vedanta's shares fell 5% following the verdict, which adds regulatory pressure amid ongoing legal challenges. The decision affects Vedanta's operational costs and has broader implications for fuel subsidy policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Vedanta shares fell after the Supreme Court upheld a ruling denying concessional diesel rates under Form C, citing misuse beyond mining purposes. The verdict adds regulatory pressure amid ongoing legal challenges and corporate developments, including its move against Adani’s Jaiprakash Associates deal and an upcoming board meeting on dividend consideration.

articles.title: New I-T Act framework notified; taxpayer relief with stricter compliance from April 1
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act has introduced new rules effective April 1, offering relief to taxpayers through increased house rent allowance limits in major cities and expanded employer-provided benefits like food coupons and cars. However, stricter disclosure requirements have been imposed on businesses and professionals, increasing compliance responsibilities for auditors and companies.
articles.raw_summary: New Income Tax Act rules are now in effect from April 1. These rules offer relief to taxpayers with increased house rent allowance limits in major cities. Employer-provided benefits like food coupons and cars are also expanded. Stricter disclosure requirements are introduced for businesses and professionals. Auditors and companies face greater responsibility in this compliance-driven regime.

articles.title: Judiciary Can't Be An Eternal Critic Or A Cheerleader : Justice Ujjal Bhuyan
articles.summary: Justice Ujjal Bhuyan of the Supreme Court stated that the judiciary must maintain its institutional balance and cannot act as a perpetual critic or supporter of the government. He emphasized that the judiciary's constitutional duty is to act as a vigilant sentinel safeguarding foundational principles. Justice Bhuyan remarked, "Judiciary must remain judiciary. Judiciary can neither be an eternal critic, nor a cheerleader. It is the sentinel on the qui vive."

articles.title: Railway Claims Tribunal Cannot Infer Death Due To Trespass Solely From Nature Of Injuries: Bombay High Court Allows Compensation
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that the Railway Claims Tribunal is not an expert body competent to conclude that death occurred due to trespass merely on the basis of the nature of injuries sustained by the deceased. This decision supports India’s renewable energy goals by facilitating large-scale clean power projects.

It also approved the power purchase agreements (PPAs) and clarified that the trading margin and implementation structure would ensure alignment with the Ministry of Power guidelines.

articles.title: Gazette notification may allow more ministries to block content
articles.summary: The government may use a gazette notification to grant multiple ministries the power to block online content under the Information Technology Act, 2000, without requiring legal amendments. Secretaries of these ministries would act as 'nodal officers' to issue such orders, expanding Section 69(A) of the Act. This move could significantly broaden the scope of content regulation in India.
articles.raw_summary: Sources told us that the government could use a gazette notification to give multiple ministries content-blocking powers under the Information Technology Act, 2000, avoiding the need for legal amendments. This would make Secretaries 'nodal officers' with authority to issue orders, expanding Section 69(A) without facing legal hurdles.

This ruling highlights the consequences of financial crimes in India. The case involves a significant amount of money and demonstrates the legal system's efforts to hold individuals accountable for their actions.

articles.title: AI Doesn't Have Qualities Of A Judge: CJI Surya Kant
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant stated that artificial intelligence (AI) lacks the qualities of a judge because it cannot understand human suffering or take responsibility for its outcomes. The remarks highlight the limitations of AI in judicial decision-making and may influence future policy discussions on AI regulation in India's legal system.
articles.raw_summary: While AI can analyse patterns and generate responses, it cannot understand human suffering or bear responsibility for its outcomes.

articles.title: Half Of Civil Litigation Can Be Shifted To Mediation In 25 Years With Right Strategies: Sriram Panchu
articles.summary: Legal expert Sriram Panchu proposed strategies to make mediation a more effective and attractive alternative to civil litigation in India. He suggested that with the right approaches, up to half of civil disputes could be resolved through mediation within 25 years. This could reduce court backlogs and improve access to justice. Panchu emphasized the need for systemic changes to promote mediation as a primary dispute resolution tool.
articles.raw_summary: Panchu outlined strategies to make mediation a more effective and attractive dispute resolution mechanism.

articles.summary: Justice Dipankar Datta shared that senior lawyers often refuse judgeship due to insufficient pay, citing an example where a lawyer declined the role because the salary couldn't cover the monthly expenses for his four pets. The issue may prompt discussions on improving judges' pay to ensure adequate talent retention in the judiciary.

articles.title: Gram Panchayat Employees Absorbed In Municipal Corporation Entitled To Equal Pay For Equal Work: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that employees absorbed from Gram Panchayats into a Municipal Corporation are entitled to parity in pay with regular employees if they perform identical duties. The Court observed that denial of equal pay for equal work in such circumstances amounts to discrimination and violates Articles 14 and 16 of the Constitution. A division bench of Justices G. S. articles.title: Subsequent Tree Growth On Land Approved For Development Won't Make It 'Deemed Forest' : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that subsequent tree growth on land already approved for development cannot be classified as a 'deemed forest.' The court emphasized that mere vegetation or tree cover alone does not constitute a natural forest ecosystem. This decision clarifies the legal interpretation of forest land and may impact environmental regulations and development projects in India.
articles.raw_summary: "Mere presence of vegetation or tree cover cannot by itself be equated with the existence of a natural forest ecosystem.”

articles.title: Judiciary Alone Can't Be Blamed For Case Pendency, Executive Also Responsible : Justice Dipankar Datta
articles.summary: Justice Dipankar Datta stated that the judiciary alone cannot be blamed for case pendency, as the executive is also responsible for delays in judicial appointments, failure to increase the judge-population ratio, and neglect of judicial infrastructure. The remarks were made in the context of addressing the backlog of cases in Indian courts.

articles.title: Insurance Society Is ‘State’ as Government Control Is Deep & Pervasive: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Air Force insurance society qualifies as a 'State' under Article 12 of the Constitution because its administration and functioning are deeply linked to government authorities. This decision establishes that the society is subject to constitutional obligations like other government entities. The ruling underscores the pervasive control of the government over such organizations.
articles.raw_summary: SC held that the Air Force insurance society qualifies as “State” under Article 12 because its administration, control, and functioning are closely linked with government authorities. articles.title: WWD 2026: Collective Groundwater Conservation Efforts in India
articles.summary: India is the world’s largest user of groundwater, with over 21.9 million wells and tubewells extracting an estimated 247 billion cubic metres annually. This invisible resource supports most of the country’s drinking water needs and sustains the livelihoods of millions of small and marginal farmers. Yet despite its centrality to India’s water security, groundwater has long been poorly understood and weakly governed.

The situation may force India to reassess its energy import strategies and contingency plans.

articles.title: US takes down ‘cybercrime-as-a-service’ botnets that hijacked over 3 million IoT devices globally

The operation underscores the global scale of IoT-based cyber threats, which pose risks to critical infrastructure and data security. India may need to strengthen its cybersecurity policies and IoT regulations in response to such transnational threats.

These botnets, operating on a 'cybercrime-as-a-service' model, were used by cybercriminals to launch attacks and extort victims for payments.

This affects large Indian IT firms and startups, but benefits highly skilled professionals like master’s degree holders in the US. The decline reflects broader challenges in securing US work visas for Indian workers.

articles.summary: LPG shortages linked to the West Asia war are pushing Indian manufacturing firms to plan energy-resilient kitchens. Companies are sourcing cylinders, using firewood, and adjusting menus while avoiding disruptions. Facility managers are preparing contingency plans, including shifting to induction and alternative equipment, amid ongoing uncertainty over fuel availability and supply continuity.

CoinDCX also warned of rising cyber fraud in India’s digital finance ecosystem.

The senators' probe highlights broader debates over corporate consolidation in the AI sector and its potential regulatory implications.

articles.title: US charges three tied to Super Micro Computer with helping smuggle billions of dollars of AI chips to China
articles.summary: US prosecutors charged three individuals, including the co-founder of Super Micro Computer, with helping smuggle billions of dollars worth of AI technology to China. The case involves allegations of violating export controls and could impact global semiconductor trade. Super Micro's shares fell sharply following the news. The incident highlights concerns over technology transfer restrictions and enforcement in the AI and semiconductor sectors.

articles.title: Whats(App) in a number? Usernames set to enter chat; global rollout likely in June
articles.summary: WhatsApp is rolling out a new feature allowing users to communicate using usernames instead of phone numbers, enhancing privacy by avoiding number sharing. The optional feature, expected globally by June 2026, lets users and businesses reserve unique handles. This change addresses growing concerns about digital privacy and data exposure in messaging platforms.

articles.title: Fresh family income survey likely ahead of CPI-IW update
articles.summary: The Labour Ministry is planning a new survey of working-class families to update the base year for the Consumer Price Index for Industrial Workers (CPI-IW) to 2025. The survey aims to capture changes in spending patterns of industrial workers. Discussions are underway to launch the survey by mid-year, which will help inform future policy decisions related to wages and inflation adjustments.

articles.title: As Iran war disrupts the Gulf, India's growth story faces new risks
articles.summary: India's economy faces new challenges. Strong growth is threatened by rising energy prices and disrupted trade. The Middle East conflict impacts oil and gas supplies crucial for India. Remittances from Indian workers abroad are also at risk. This situation could lead to slower growth, higher inflation, and a weaker currency for India.

articles.summary: India faces serious energy security risks due to disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz. LPG supplies are particularly vulnerable, with a large percentage of imports transiting this vital waterway. Damage to refining infrastructure and logistical challenges could lead to prolonged shortages of refined products. India's crude oil reserves offer limited protection. Alternative shipping routes also present constraints.

Telangana's digital approach is modernising urban administration.

articles.summary: The finance ministry in a statement said special provisions have been incorporated in the scheme for exporters, which includes a guaranteed loan of Rs 20 crore for eligible exporters having profitable units who have earned at least a quarter of their revenues from exports in each of the last three financial years, and satisfied certain export realisation conditions.

articles.summary: A growing Middle East conflict poses a risk of fresh global inflation. SBI Research reports that supply chains and asset classes could be affected. However, India's economy may face limited impact. Diversified crude oil imports and policy measures are expected to cushion the effects. Remittances from Gulf countries and crude oil prices remain key considerations for India.

articles.summary: American buyers have stopped contacting Indian spice exporters. This is due to the Iran war. This trade disruption affects India's valuable farm exports. The US is India's largest spice market. This situation could lead to excess domestic supply and lower prices. Exporters and farmers face increased stress. Trade routes are disrupted, and freight costs are high.

articles.title: India's core growth eases to 2.3% in February on poor energy show
articles.summary: India's core sector growth decelerated to a three-month low of 2.3% in February, impacted by subdued electricity generation and declining energy industries. Cement and steel showed growth, driven by government infrastructure initiatives, but overall industrial output is expected to face headwinds from the West Asia crisis.

articles.title: Govt amends norms for exporters availing the interest subvention support
articles.summary: The Indian government has amended rules for exporters receiving interest subvention on credit loans, effective January 2. Exporters will lose benefits if their loans become Non-Performing Assets, and early repayments will only qualify for subvention on the actual outstanding period. Digital processes have been introduced to streamline claims. The changes aim to improve efficiency in trade financing and reduce misuse of government support.

These changes aim to streamline benefits for trade and banks.

articles.summary: The technology involves ovum pick-up, in vitro fertilization, and embryo transfer. This opens new avenues for rapid genetic improvement of India's dairy breeds.

articles.title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass to resume after six years
articles.summary: Border trade between India and China via Lipulekh Pass in Pithoragarh is set to resume. Preparations are underway for the trade session, typically from June to September. This move follows government directives and clearances. Traders can retrieve goods stored in Tibet. The resumption aims for better coordination and facilitation.

articles.title: Government amends guidelines for interest subvention support for export credit
articles.summary: The government has amended guidelines for interest subvention support on export credit loans. Under the new rules, interest subvention will no longer be available once a loan account is classified as a non-performing asset (NPA). This change aims to ensure fiscal discipline in export credit financing.

articles.title: Anti dumping duty suggested on spandex, raw material for foam imported from China, Vietnam
articles.summary: India's Directorate General of Trade Remedies has recommended anti-dumping duties for five years on spandex and flexible slabstock polyol imports. These duties target products from China and Vietnam. The recommendations follow investigations into complaints about dumped goods hurting local manufacturers. The proposed duties aim to protect domestic industries from unfair pricing.

With the upcoming Income Tax Act, effective April 1, 2025, the goal is to transition India into a more tax-friendly environment.

articles.title: New Income Tax Act 2025 to prioritize simplicity and stakeholder engagement, says FM Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman announced the Income Tax Act 2025, which will replace the 1961 Act with a simpler framework effective April 1, 2026. The new law aims to improve taxpayer compliance through stakeholder engagement and a collaborative approach. The government will prioritize simplicity and partnership over adversarial tax relationships.

articles.title: Core sector output eased to a three-month low of 2.3% in February
articles.summary: India's core sector growth decelerated to a three-month low of 2.3% in February, impacted by weaker electricity generation and energy industry declines. This slowdown signals a broader economic moderation, with forecasts adjusted due to persistent energy price concerns.

articles.summary: India is now treating vital energy data as a national security matter. All oil and gas companies must share detailed operational information. This move aims to strengthen government oversight and improve responses to supply disruptions. Real-time monitoring of energy supplies will help prioritize critical sectors and make better procurement decisions during crises. This ensures India's energy security.

Global oil prices and the Iran conflict contributed to this pressure.

articles.title: Global trade growth to slow to 1.4-1.9%, Middle East conflict a risk: WTO
articles.summary: The World Trade Organization (WTO) has lowered its forecast for global trade growth in 2024 to 1.4-1.9%, down from last year's surge driven by AI-related goods and inventory preloading to avoid tariffs. Ongoing conflicts in the Middle East pose additional risks to trade stability. This slowdown could impact India's export-driven economy and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: The forecast for global trade this year is less optimistic, as the World Trade Organization projects an increase of merely 1.9 percent in merchandise trade. Ongoing tensions in the Middle East could exacerbate these challenges. Last year's growth was markedly influenced by the surge in AI-related goods and a strategic approach to preloading inventory to circumvent tariffs.

articles.title: Farmers seek agri, dairy carve-out in India-US trade deal over duty-free import fears
articles.summary: In a fervent display of solidarity, farmers have rallied against key components of the proposed India-US trade agreement, fearing that duty-free imports could devastate local agriculture. The throngs at Ramlila Maidan echoed their concerns as they insisted on a legal guarantee for the Minimum Support Price (MSP), citing the Swaminathan Commission as a benchmark.
articles.raw_summary: In a fervent display of solidarity, farmers have rallied against key components of the proposed India-US trade agreement, fearing that duty-free imports could devastate local agriculture. The throngs at Ramlila Maidan echoed their concerns as they insisted on a legal guarantee for the Minimum Support Price (MSP), citing the Swaminathan Commission as a benchmark.

articles.title: 5 key issues at upcoming WTO meet, India to play constructive role: Official
articles.summary: The World Trade Organization's ministerial conference is set to discuss key global trade issues. Topics include e-commerce, agriculture, fisheries, and investment facilitation. India will lead discussions on these matters. The conference will also consider reforms for the WTO. Potential discussions on US tariffs are also on the agenda. India aims to play a constructive role.

India aims to play a constructive role.

articles.title: India recommends anti-dumping duty on Chinese chemical used in dye industry
articles.summary: India's trade ministry has recommended imposing anti-dumping duties on a key industrial chemical imported from China. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies found that low-priced shipments were harming domestic producers. The proposed duties aim to address dumped prices causing injury to Indian manufacturers. The finance ministry will make the final decision on this matter.

The finance ministry will make the final decision on this matter.

articles.title: NITI Aayog lays out a roadmap to make India global manufacturing hub for high quality sports goods
articles.summary: In its report ‘Realising the Export Potential of India’s Sports Equipment Manufacturing Sector’, released on Thursday, the Aayog has proposed a seven-pronged strategy that includes structural reforms and fiscal incentives to the tune of Rs 7500 crore from 2027-2031 to help scale up manufacturing and exports of sports goods and equipment from the country.

articles.title: India in talks with Russia, Belarus, Morocco to boost fertiliser imports, sources say
articles.summary: India is in talks to boost fertilizer imports from Russia, Belarus, and Morocco due to Middle East tensions and China's export curbs. With the summer planting season approaching, the nation is seeking to secure supplies of urea and DAP, its most crucial fertilizers, to avert potential shortfalls.

articles.title: India's oilmeal export to China jumps 20-fold to 7.79 lakh tons in 11 months of FY26: SEA
articles.summary: India's oilmeal exports to China saw a massive jump this fiscal year. Competitive prices and trade policies favored Indian products. However, this advantage is now challenged. Meanwhile, overall oilmeal exports have declined. Geopolitical conflicts are disrupting shipments to the Middle East and Europe, increasing transit times and costs. This situation threatens India's export competitiveness in key markets.

articles.summary: Indian markets are navigating a complex landscape. Global energy disruptions are impacting exporters directly. A domestic crisis at HDFC Bank, stemming from corporate governance issues, is also weighing on investor sentiment. Despite the gloom, defence, infrastructure, and food sectors offer potential resilience. Investors are closely watching oil prices and the rupee's trajectory as economic challenges loom.

articles.title: Gold extends fall after worst week in 43 years. More pain or time to buy the dip?
articles.summary: Gold prices fell further after the worst week in 43 years, driven by rising oil prices and expectations of higher interest rates. Geopolitical tensions and warnings from Donald Trump added to market volatility. Analysts from Motilal Oswal and IndusInd Securities noted weakening momentum despite gold's safe-haven appeal.

articles.title: Sector To Watch: Waaree, Premier Energies seen key beneficiaries of solar integration push: Siddhartha Khemka
articles.summary: India’s solar manufacturing sector is set for structural change as ALMM expands to include wafers, driving backward integration and consolidation. Analysts see Waaree Energies and Premier Energies as key beneficiaries due to strong capacities and integration strategies, with policy support and localisation efforts expected to boost long-term growth and competitiveness.

articles.title: Rupee hits all-time low of 93.84 vs USD amid Mideast tensions
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit a record low against the U.S. dollar amid escalating Middle East conflict, raising concerns about energy supply disruptions and impacting Asia's third-largest economy. Asian currencies also declined as geopolitical tensions intensified, with oil prices surging over 50% this month.

articles.title: Gold braces for worst week in 4 decades
articles.summary: Gold prices are set for their worst weekly decline in 40 years due to rising energy costs and reduced expectations of interest-rate cuts. The decline was driven by a stronger dollar and bond yields, amid concerns over escalating conflicts in West Asia and potential inflation. The article highlights how geopolitical tensions and global economic shifts are impacting commodity markets.
articles.raw_summary: Gold is poised for its largest weekly loss since 1983, driven by rising energy prices and diminished expectations for interest-rate cuts. The precious metal's decline accelerated as the dollar and bond yields rallied amid concerns of escalating conflict in West Asia and potential inflation.

articles.title: Kotak Bank set to acquire Deutsche's retail business in Rs 4,500-crore deal
articles.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank is poised to take a major leap by acquiring Deutsche Bank's retail operations in India for around Rs 4,500 crore. This deal promises to strengthen Kotak's retail lending capabilities and broaden its base of deposits. Meanwhile, Deutsche Bank's exit reflects a larger strategy to streamline its operations worldwide.

articles.title: Gujarat-based company develops 'AI Action Firewall' to make artificial intelligence systems safer
articles.summary: A Gujarat-based company, NeuroPause Lab Limited, has developed an 'AI Action Firewall' designed to enhance the safety of artificial intelligence systems. The firewall acts as a policy-based safety layer, monitoring and controlling AI actions to ensure they are authorized, monitored, and recorded. Saurabh Patel, the founder and CEO, stated that this system will function between AI systems and real-world operations to mitigate risks. The development underscores growing concerns about AI safety and
articles.raw_summary: A firewall is a network security system for monitoring and controlling incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predetermined security rules. The firewall will function as a policy-based safety layer between AI systems and real-world operations, ensuring that actions are authorised, monitored and recorded, Saurabh Patel, founder and CEO of NeuroPause Lab Limited said.

articles.summary: The divestment of IDBI Bank is facing delays due to disagreements over valuation and deal structure. Experts cite the large transaction size, investor demand for management control, and multiple stakeholders as key challenges. Similar deals in private banks have been completed, but alignment on pricing and clarity on terms are critical for progress. The article is authored by Abizer Diwanji and sourced from CNBC Economy.
articles.raw_summary: Experts point to the large transaction size, investor demand for management control and the involvement of multiple stakeholders as key factors. articles.title: Iran conflict could push up inflation, weigh on global growth: IMF
articles.summary: The IMF warns that escalating conflict in Iran could push global oil prices above $100 per barrel, potentially raising global inflation by 2% and reducing economic output by 1%. IMF spokesperson Julie Kozack highlighted these risks but noted no immediate aid requests have been made. The situation could impact India's energy security and economic stability, given its reliance on oil imports.

articles.summary: The World Trade Organization (WTO) has projected global merchandise trade growth to slow to 1.9% in 2026, citing risks from tensions in West Asia, including potential disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. These geopolitical risks could affect goods and services trade flows, which may have implications for India's trade policies and economic stability. The WTO warns that such disruptions could pose downside risks to global trade.

articles.title: Govt ends airfare caps from March 23 as global pressures mount on aviation sector
articles.summary: The Indian government will remove airfare caps starting March 23, 2026, ending price controls introduced in December 2025 amid widespread flight cancellations by IndiGo that caused ticket prices to surge. The move addresses global pressures on the aviation sector and follows the disruption caused by the airline's operational issues. The decision marks a shift in regulatory oversight of air travel pricing.
articles.raw_summary: The fare caps, imposed in early December 2025, were introduced at the height of the IndiGo disruption when widespread flight cancellations triggered an unprecedented surge in ticket prices across routes.

articles.summary: Under the policy, a BMI range of 18 to 24.9 is classified as normal and set as the desired standard for cabin crew members. Those falling within this range will be considered fit to operate flights without additional requirements.

articles.summary: Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal highlighted three major challenges in India's mining sector: delays in land acquisition, slow environmental and forest clearances, and high auction premiums that make some mines unviable. He urged faster post-auction execution to strengthen the country's energy and mineral security amid geopolitical risks. The concerns point to systemic issues that could require policy reforms to address.

articles.title: EXCLUSIVE: HDFC Bank fires group head of branch banking, two others —  here’s why
articles.summary: HDFC Bank terminated three senior executives—Sampath Kumar, Harsh Gupta, and Payal Mandhyan—allegedly for mis-selling Credit Suisse AT-1 bonds and misleading NRI clients into transferring FCNR deposits to Bahrain. Oil prices have crossed $100 per barrel, raising concerns about energy security and economic impact. The recommendations aim to reduce oil demand amid supply constraints.

articles.summary: Super Micro Computer co-founder Yih-Shyan 'Wally' Liaw has been accused of smuggling AI servers to China, violating US export controls. Liaw and others face up to 20 years in prison if convicted. The case led to a 14% drop in Super Micro's stock price. The incident raises concerns about enforcement of technology export restrictions, which could impact global supply chains, including those involving India.

The report highlights the Iran-Israel conflict and uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz as key risk factors. Brent crude prices could rise above $100 per barrel. This could impact India's energy imports, inflation, and trade policies.

The decision reflects a government effort to address regulatory requirements in the banking sector.

The company's manufacturing activities requiring LPG have been halted, raising concerns about energy supply vulnerabilities for industries in India. LPG is a critical input for many industrial processes, and disruptions can impact production and economic activity.
articles.raw_summary: IFGL Refractories Ltd has temporarily halted operations requiring liquified petroleum gas (LPG) at its Kandla manufacturing facility in Gujarat due to disruption in LPG supply amid the ongoing conflict in the Gulf region.

articles.title: Artificial Intelligence must strengthen, not override judiciary, says CJI Justice Surya Kant
articles.summary: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Justice Surya Kant stated that Artificial Intelligence (AI) should support the judiciary by managing large data volumes, identifying patterns, and reducing delays. However, he emphasized that AI must not replace the core judicial function of delivering judgments. The remarks were made in the context of leveraging technology to improve efficiency in India's legal system.
articles.raw_summary: ”AI should assist in handling large volumes of data and records, identifying patterns, and reducing procedural delays. However, it must not encroach upon the core judicial function of delivering judgments,” the CJI was quoted as saying.

articles.summary: The Indian government will increase LPG supply to states by 20% starting March 23, 2026, with additional allocations for hotels, restaurants, dhabas, and other commercial and industrial sectors. The decision aims to address supply needs in these sectors, though the exact rationale for the timing or demand drivers is not specified in the report.
articles.raw_summary: The additional supply will be mainly for restaurants, dhabas, hotels and other commercial and industrial sectors. The additional allocation will come into effect from March 23, 2026, and will continue until further notice.

ISRO confirmed the debris originated from one of its rockets, prompting an investigation into its origin and safety implications. The incident raises questions about space debris management and regulatory oversight in India's space activities.

articles.title: Supreme Court to hear plea on Gurugram 4-year-old rape, no arrests yet
articles.summary: The Supreme Court agreed to hear a petition seeking a Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) or Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe into the alleged rape of a 4-year-old child in Gurugram. The petitioner alleged police inaction and no arrests have been made in the case. The court's decision to hear the plea reflects concerns over the handling of such crimes and may prompt further legal or policy action regarding child protection and law enforcement accountability.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court agreed to hear a plea seeking a CBI or SIT probe into the alleged rape of a 4-year-old in Gurugram, with the petitioner alleging police inaction and no arrests so far.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed Gujarat's plea against granting bail to Rakshit Ravish Chorasiya, who was involved in a 2025 Vadodara car crash that killed Hemali Patel. articles.summary: Researchers have discovered a spyware tool called DarkSword that exploits vulnerabilities in Apple's Safari browser to target iPhones. The malware can expose user data within seconds, putting up to 270 million devices at risk if not updated. Experts warn that this poses a major cybersecurity threat, particularly for users in India where smartphone penetration is high. The incident highlights the need for stronger digital security measures and user awareness.
articles.raw_summary: A newly discovered spyware tool, DarkSword, is targeting iPhones through Safari exploits, exposing data within seconds. Experts warn that up to 270 million devices may be at risk if not updated.

articles.title: DPDP Rules | How to achieve real compliance when intent isn't enough
articles.summary: India's Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Rules have come into force, shifting compliance focus from declarations to verifiable actions. Enterprises now face the challenge of proving compliance rather than just stating intent. The article outlines ten steps to help businesses navigate these new requirements, emphasizing the need for robust documentation and evidence-based adherence to the rules.
articles.raw_summary: With the DPDP Rules now in force, compliance is no longer about what you say—it’s about what you can prove. For enterprises, the real challenge might just have begun. These ten steps can help.

articles.title: EPFO reaches out to subscribers with unclaimed balance

The initiative aims to clear idle funds and improve ease of access to entitled benefits.

articles.title: With 7th regiment, Army expands Pinaka fleet
articles.summary: The Indian Army has inducted a 7th regiment equipped with Pinaka multi-barrel rocket launchers, expanding its fleet of this indigenously developed artillery system. The Pinaka is a key part of India's artillery modernization efforts, enhancing long-range strike capabilities. This move strengthens the Army's firepower and aligns with ongoing efforts to bolster indigenous defence production under the 'Make in India' initiative.

articles.title: CAFE 3.0 rollout: Penalty recovery for carmakers remains a grey area
articles.summary: Government seeks clarity on recovering hefty penalties from carmakers for missing fuel efficiency targets. With stricter norms ahead, the PMO is pushing ministries to define who evaluates, approves, and recovers the dues. Over Rs 8,800 crore in penalties have been levied for past non-compliance, yet no recovery has occurred.

articles.title: Kilometrage allowance hiked by 25% for train crew
articles.summary: Indian Railways has boosted Kilometrage Allowance and Allowance in lieu of Kilometrage by 25% for over two lakh running staff, effective January 2024. This decision aligns compensation with the 50% Dearness Allowance hike, addressing long-standing demands for better financial benefits for essential operational personnel.

articles.title: Maoist endgame nears, but no political fanfare in offing
articles.summary: The March 31 deadline for eradicating Left-wing extremism in India may pass without official celebration, as security operations have led to the neutralization or surrender of most Maoist leaders. This has fractured the CPI(Maoist) group, pushing remaining members toward surrender. The psychological pressure from the deadline has accelerated the group's decline, though no formal political announcement is expected. The focus remains on sustaining these gains without fanfare.

articles.title: UAPA misuse not a model for Viksit Bharat: SC judge
articles.summary: SC judge Ujjal Bhuyan emphasized that a developed India by 2047 requires greater tolerance for debate and dissent, without criminalizing them. articles.title: PM Modi is India's longest serving head of govt
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has become the longest-serving head of government, surpassing the record previously held by Pawan Kumar Chamling. This milestone marks 25 continuous years in leadership, beginning with his tenure as Gujarat Chief Minister in October 2001 and continuing as Prime Minister since May 2014.

articles.title: DICGC risk-based premium: How much will banks pay for deposit insurance?
articles.summary: The Deposit Insurance and Credit Guarantee Corporation (DICGC) has introduced a risk-based premium (RBP) framework for banks, offering discounts of up to 33.33% on deposit insurance premiums based on a bank's rating category. Banks may also receive an additional 25% incentive for long-term stability (vintage). This move aims to align insurance costs with risk levels, potentially reducing financial burdens for well-rated banks while ensuring stronger regulatory oversight of the banking sector.

articles.title: Explained: The benefits of DGCA's new flight ticket refund rules
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has introduced new rules for flight ticket refunds and amendments. Passengers can now cancel or amend tickets without charges within 48 hours of booking, and correct passenger names without fees within 24 hours. These changes aim to enhance consumer protection in India's aviation sector.
articles.raw_summary: There's now a 48-hour look-in period for flight ticket cancellation or amendment with no charges, and a 24-hour window for name correction without charges.
